current_palisade_state.current_state_summary: Palisade owns a versioned repository policy artifact and validator. The artifact is not consumed by public copy, runtime routes, Conduit execution, Vault/NEXUS execution, model API execution, Weave orchestration, or operational release authority.

non_claims: This map does not implement runtime enforcement.
This map does not deploy Palisade as an enforcement membrane.
This map does not create a production policy service.
This map does not create production workspace maturity.
This map does not expose public NEXUS runtime execution.
This map does not create model API execution.
This map does not create operational release authority.
This map does not implement Weave orchestration.
This map does not modify public routes or public-facing copy.

required_input_mapping.source: policy bundle schemas and test cases

required_decision_mapping.use: Continue proving the policy artifact is internally evaluable before external consumers depend on it.

implementation_preconditions: Keep the existing policy bundle validation command passing.
Do not change policy semantics during consumer integration.
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Low for validation self-checks, but it does not move public claim controls or repository contracts toward policy consumption by itself.
candidate_consumers.rationale: The validator already evaluates the policy artifact. It should remain the source of policy integrity, but making it the first external consumer would not add a new consumption path.

required_input_mapping.evidence_state: finding evidence and missing production/runtime components
required_input_mapping.operator_authorization_state: operator review queue state when present

required_decision_mapping.decision: deny or requires_evidence must preserve current unclaimable boundaries
required_decision_mapping.required_evidence: must remain visible in finding requirements
required_decision_mapping.missing_evidence: must explain why claims remain blocked
required_decision_mapping.runtime_enforcement_status: must remain unavailable for current audit state

implementation_preconditions: Define a reusable Palisade decision reader before the audit validator consumes policy outputs.
Keep the audit record historical where it describes its original inspection baseline.
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Moderate. The audit contains historical baseline statements, so direct mutation could blur historical evidence with current policy consumption.
candidate_consumers.rationale: The audit already mirrors Palisade thresholds, but its historical character makes product-language validation a cleaner first consumer.

required_input_mapping.surface: public copy file or data file being scanned
required_input_mapping.claim_id: claim detected by phrase or configured negative-space mapping
required_input_mapping.requested_action: claim_public_capability, expose_public_runtime_execution, claim_model_api_execution, claim_operational_release_authority, or advance_surface_to_product_language
required_input_mapping.evidence_state: negative-space mapping evidence plus missing evidence from policy thresholds
required_input_mapping.production_workspace_threshold_state: all incomplete unless a future evidence record marks every threshold component exists and verified
required_input_mapping.runtime_governance_path_state: all incomplete unless a future evidence record marks every runtime path component exists and verified
required_input_mapping.operator_authorization_state: not_requested unless an Operator review record is explicitly referenced
required_input_mapping.current_repository_state_basis: validator target file path and source data path

required_decision_mapping.decision: deny, requires_evidence, or requires_operator_review determines whether the phrase is allowed, blocked, or must cite review
required_decision_mapping.allowed: true may permit stronger claim language only under complete threshold evidence
required_decision_mapping.required_evidence: reported as construction targets when a phrase is blocked
required_decision_mapping.operator_review_required: blocks threshold transitions unless approved evidence is present
required_decision_mapping.runtime_enforcement_status: must be unavailable for current repository state

implementation_preconditions: Add a local policy-decision reader for the Palisade JSON bundle.
Map existing forbidden claim patterns and negative-space mappings to Palisade claim_id values.
Fail closed when the policy bundle, schemas, or required decisions are missing.
Keep public-copy restrictions at least as strict as the current validator.
Do not change public routes or public-facing copy.
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Low if implemented as validator-only consumption. The main risk is accidentally treating policy evaluation as runtime enforcement, which the contract must forbid.
candidate_consumers.rationale: This validator already gates the claim language Palisade is designed to decide. It can consume policy decisions in one bounded implementation pass without runtime wiring, public copy changes, Conduit execution, Vault/NEXUS execution, model calls, or Weave orchestration.

required_input_mapping.claim_id: claim pattern id mapped to Palisade claim_id
required_input_mapping.evidence_state: restricted claim pattern and required boundary phrase coverage
required_input_mapping.operator_authorization_state: not_requested unless a review record is present

required_decision_mapping.decision: deny must keep restricted claims blocked
required_decision_mapping.missing_evidence: must preserve README current-state truth reasons

implementation_preconditions: Complete product-language validator consumption first so README-specific checks reuse the same policy reader.
Keep README content unchanged unless separately authorized.
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Moderate. README checks are narrower and should inherit the product-language policy adapter rather than creating a second mapping surface first.
candidate_consumers.rationale: README truth checks are a good second validator consumer after the shared claim-language policy adapter exists.

required_input_mapping.evidence_state: inventory classification, public surface status, and risk flags

required_decision_mapping.decision: requires_operator_review may preserve review queue routing
required_decision_mapping.required_evidence: may become inventory evidence snippets after claim-language consumption exists
required_decision_mapping.missing_evidence: may drive risk flags only after contract alignment

implementation_preconditions: Do not promote Palisade internal artifacts through public navigation metadata.
Only add inventory recognition if an existing validator requires it.
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Moderate. It would mostly classify artifacts and could become another mapping surface rather than an implementation path.
candidate_consumers.rationale: Inventory validators matter for repository hygiene, but they are not the first path from policy decision to claim control.

required_input_mapping.evidence_state: required evidence classes and disallowed claims

required_decision_mapping.decision: deny must preserve operational-use and release-authority denial
required_decision_mapping.required_evidence: must remain equal to required evidence classes
required_decision_mapping.missing_evidence: must remain visible in claim effects

implementation_preconditions: Product-language validator must consume Palisade decisions first.
Operational evidence contract must keep non-implementation boundary flags false.
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Moderate. It is close to release authority language and must not imply operational evidence currently exists.
candidate_consumers.rationale: This contract should consume Palisade after claim-language control is policy-backed, because it is nearer to operational authority claims.

required_input_mapping.surface: Direct UI or protected workspace validator target
required_input_mapping.evidence_state: auth, callback, protected-shell, and bounded persistence evidence
required_input_mapping.production_workspace_threshold_state: provider login, callback, route admission, and bounded persistence remain partial or absent for production maturity
required_input_mapping.operator_authorization_state: not_requested unless a review record is attached
required_input_mapping.current_repository_state_basis: Direct UI data artifact and validator path

required_decision_mapping.decision: deny must keep production workspace claim blocked
required_decision_mapping.required_evidence: must preserve production workspace threshold components
required_decision_mapping.missing_evidence: must explain which workspace threshold components are absent

implementation_preconditions: Do not change auth/session guards or protected-route behavior.
Do not treat bounded persistence as production workspace maturity.
Complete claim-language validator consumption first.
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: High if misread as runtime gating for protected routes. Safe consumption requires a prior validator-only policy adapter.
candidate_consumers.rationale: Direct UI validators are important, but first consumption should avoid touching auth/protected behavior.

candidate_consumers.current_location: data/docs.json, data/interface-fixtures.v0.json, data/interface-fixture.example.v0.json, and public route data sources

required_input_mapping.claim_id: claim implied by state label or fixture evidence requirement
required_input_mapping.evidence_state: public state metadata and fixture evidence requirements
required_input_mapping.production_workspace_threshold_state: not ready for direct public-state consumption
required_input_mapping.runtime_governance_path_state: not ready for direct public-state consumption

required_decision_mapping.decision: must not drive public text until validator consumption is implemented
required_decision_mapping.missing_evidence: must remain validator-only before public state mutation

implementation_preconditions: Policy-backed validator consumption must exist first.
Any public state change must be separately authorized.
forbidden_overclaims: public state data is a runtime decision
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: High. Direct public-state mutation would risk public copy changes and premature product language.
candidate_consumers.rationale: Public state data should reflect policy only after validators consume policy decisions and preserve no-overclaim behavior.

required_input_mapping.evidence_state: configured claim pattern, evidence mappings, and denied claim list

required_decision_mapping.decision: deny blocks claim, requires_evidence preserves construction requirement, requires_operator_review blocks transition
required_decision_mapping.allowed: permits claim only after threshold evidence is complete
required_decision_mapping.runtime_enforcement_status: must not be treated as runtime enforcement

implementation_preconditions: Create one local policy consumption adapter used by claim-language validators.
Keep existing forbidden phrase coverage intact.
forbidden_overclaims: validator-backed claim language equals runtime enforcement
policy allowed fixture equals current repository state
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Low when scoped to validator-only consumption and no public-copy edits.
candidate_consumers.rationale: Claim-language checks are the functional category of the selected first consumer.

required_input_mapping.evidence_state: Conduit contract boundary flags and stop conditions
required_input_mapping.runtime_governance_path_state: Conduit exists as contract evidence only; runtime path remains incomplete

required_decision_mapping.decision: requires_evidence must preserve contract-only status until runtime path components exist

implementation_preconditions: Complete claim-language validator consumption first.
Any Conduit contract alignment must be contract-only and separately authorized.
No Conduit runtime behavior may be changed.
forbidden_overclaims: Conduit is wired to public runtime input
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: High if pursued as runtime integration; acceptable only as a contract-readiness consumer after claim-language controls are policy-backed.
candidate_consumers.rationale: Conduit is within the two-to-three pass horizon, but it should consume policy through contract readiness after the first validator consumer proves the policy adapter.

candidate_consumers.current_location: No deployed runtime release-state path exists; related evidence appears in public-surface audit, operational evidence contract, and Track 3 local contracts.

required_input_mapping.production_workspace_threshold_state: must be complete before production workspace claims
required_input_mapping.runtime_governance_path_state: must mark every path component exists and verified before allow

required_decision_mapping.decision: allow only after complete governed path evidence
required_decision_mapping.required_evidence: must be persisted as evidence record
required_decision_mapping.runtime_enforcement_status: must be available only after deployed enforcement is separately authorized and verified

implementation_preconditions: production runtime enforcement authorization
durable evidence/audit record
release-state decision capability
tenant and workspace maturity where user/workspace input is involved
Conduit/Vault contract alignment
forbidden_overclaims: runtime release-state decision path is operational
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Blocking risk. This would require runtime enforcement, release authority, and durable audit evidence that do not exist.
candidate_consumers.rationale: This is the long-horizon target, not the first consumer.

candidate_consumers.current_location: Specification references only; no Weave runtime path is present.

required_decision_mapping.decision: requires_operator_review or deny until authorized implementation evidence exists

implementation_preconditions: explicit Operator authorization
orchestration runtime implementation
runtime governance path implementation
durable evidence/audit record
candidate_consumers.risk_if_integrated_now: Blocking risk. Weave orchestration would require authorization and runtime work outside this pass.
candidate_consumers.rationale: Weave must not consume Palisade policy until an authorized orchestration implementation path exists.

consumer_priority_order.reason: First bounded implementation path: validator-only policy consumption for public claim language without public copy changes.
Shared claim-language category that should reuse the product-language validator policy adapter.
README current-state truth can consume the same policy adapter after product-language validation proves the pattern.
Operational evidence and release-authority boundaries should consume policy only after claim language is policy-backed.
Conduit contract readiness is within the near horizon but must remain contract-only and authorized.

product_language_boundary_validator.required_evidence_handling: Report required_evidence as construction targets and keep existing phrase bans active until policy decision is allow.
product_language_boundary_validator.missing_evidence_handling: Fail the validator on claim language that lacks the evidence required by the Palisade decision.
product_language_boundary_validator.operator_review_required_handling: Fail closed unless an explicit Operator approval evidence record is provided by an authorized future pass.
product_language_boundary_validator.runtime_enforcement_status_handling: Treat unavailable as proof that validator consumption is not runtime enforcement.
product_language_boundary_validator.fail_closed_behavior: If policy bundle, schemas, decision outputs, or claim mappings are missing or malformed, fail validation.
product_language_boundary_validator.no_overclaim_behavior: Never convert a policy test fixture or repository validator pass into a public claim of runtime enforcement, production workspace maturity, public NEXUS/model execution, or operational release authority.

blocked_consumers.reason: Direct public-state mutation requires policy-backed validator consumption first and separate authorization for public-facing changes.
Requires production runtime enforcement, durable evidence/audit record, release-state decision capability, and operational authorization.
Requires explicit Operator authorization and a real orchestration runtime path.

bounded_next_pass_contract.required_evidence_handling: Use policy required_evidence to keep construction targets explicit for blocked or evidence-seeking claims.
bounded_next_pass_contract.missing_evidence_handling: Use policy missing_evidence to explain validator failures for production workspace, public runtime, model execution, and release-authority language.
bounded_next_pass_contract.operator_review_required_handling: Treat operator_review_required true as a hard validator failure unless an authorized Operator approval record is explicitly mapped.
bounded_next_pass_contract.runtime_enforcement_status_handling: Require runtime_enforcement_status unavailable for current repository state and reject any interpretation that validator consumption is runtime enforcement.
bounded_next_pass_contract.fail_closed_behavior: Fail validation if policy files cannot be read, required decisions are absent, or a claim phrase cannot be mapped to a Palisade claim_id.
bounded_next_pass_contract.no_overclaim_behavior: Do not alter public copy and do not treat policy allow fixtures as current repository evidence.

first_recommended_consumer.rationale: The product-language validator already owns the strongest public claim guardrail. Making it consume Palisade decisions is a bounded implementation pass that moves claim control from hard-coded stated limits toward policy-owned decisions without runtime enforcement or public surface changes.

required_next_pass.name: Implement Palisade-backed product-language validator consumption
scope: map product-language claim patterns to Palisade claim_id values
fail closed when policy consumption cannot decide

implementation_risks.risk: Validator-only policy consumption could be mistaken for runtime enforcement.
implementation_risks.mitigation: Require runtime_enforcement_status unavailable for current repository state and keep no-overclaim checks active.

implementation_risks.risk: Claim phrase mappings could weaken existing product-language restrictions.
implementation_risks.mitigation: Fail closed on unmapped claims and keep existing forbidden-pattern checks at least as strict.

implementation_risks.risk: Policy test fixtures could be misread as current repository evidence.
implementation_risks.mitigation: Reject any current-state allow claim unless real threshold evidence exists outside hypothetical policy test fixtures.

runtime_enforcement_boundary.boundary_statement: The map defines repository validator consumption order only. It does not make Palisade operational.

evidence_required_before_runtime_enforce: deployed Palisade policy service or authorized equivalent
Conduit contract and runtime normalization path
production workspace threshold evidence where workspace claims are involved

components.basis: Browser-side and protected-shell interactions exist, but no governed runtime input path exists.
Static public Facade surfaces exist and validators preserve claim boundaries.
Track 3 Conduit contracts and local validators exist as repository-local evidence, not public runtime wiring.
The policy bundle defines evaluable decisions, but no runtime caller exists.
Vault/NEXUS contracts, fixtures, and local adapter evidence exist, but public runtime execution is not implemented.
Operational evidence contract and workspace event substrate records exist, but no durable production audit trail is wired into runtime governance.
No operational release-state decision capability exists.
Public surfaces can display bounded static results, but no governed runtime result is surfaced.

runtime_governance_path_assessment.sufficiency_reason: The governed runtime path is sufficient only when every required path component exists and is verified. Current components are partial, policy-represented only, stubbed, absent, or unverified.

production_workspace_threshold_relevance.threshold_source: palisade/policy-bundle.v0/manifest.json and README current-state truth section
